text: The present study aims to examine the relationship between emotional intelligence and psychological readiness for marriage in young men. Mental maturity for marriage is defined as a structure that includes three basic components for long-term relationships as self-acceptance, inner integration and stable marital and family orientation. For this purpose, 90 male university students were applied to the study. The findings revealed that the young men who have higher psychological maturity for marriage have higher emotional intelligence than those who have lower psychological maturity. In the study, it was seen that the young men who have higher psychological maturity for marriage are better in controlling their emotions for solving problems and adapting to life situations.
